items.prompt: interprets limits and derivative-based behavior across tables, graphs, formulas, and bounded motion or optimization contexts
items.expected_evidence: interpret approaching behavior consistently across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner treats limits and derivative-based evidence as coherent behavior reasoning rather than as disconnected procedural steps.

items.prompt: explains how derivative meaning links slope, rate of change, motion, and local linear behavior in introductory calculus contexts
items.expected_evidence: explain derivative meaning through slope, rate, motion, and local linearization rather than only through symbolic rules
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains derivative meaning as one coherent local-change idea across multiple representations.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to symbolic_graphical_motion_disconnect.
items.expected_evidence: connect derivative sign, magnitude, and change to increasing, decreasing, and extremum behavior
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where symbolic derivative work stopped matching graph or motion meaning.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar introductory calculus context and identifies which local or rate-based evidence governs the claim
items.expected_evidence: justify calculus claims in bounded modeling or analysis tasks using the correct local or rate-based evidence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent limit and derivative reasoning in a varied context and names the evidence that actually controls the conclusion.
